Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 14:04 ET
Single-name calls surge despite index put-heavy positioning ahead of NFP
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Stark positioning divergence emerges with index P/C at 1.36 versus single-name P/C at 0.87 — classic dispersion hedge setup before NFP.
  • 2. SPY negative gamma at -$2.5B amplifies any NFP reaction while QQQ positive gamma provides tech stability buffer.
  • 3. Energy leads with XLE golden cross and massive call blocks as oil vol remains elevated at 28% above equity vol.
NARRATIVE — dispersion setup pre-NFP

The most striking signal is the positioning split between indices and single names, with index put demand (P/C 1.36) contrasting sharply against single-name call accumulation (P/C 0.87) — a 0.49 divergence that screams institutional dispersion hedging into tomorrow's NFP. This structure suggests funds are protecting broad portfolios while expressing bullish single-name conviction, amplified by implied correlation at just 0.18 indicating maximum stock-specific opportunity. Energy dominance stands out across multiple layers, with XLE recording both the strongest technical score (84.4) and massive institutional call accumulation — 15,000 May 64C contracts at $0.74 representing $11M in premium. The sector's golden cross and RSI overbought condition at 70 aligns with vol surface signals showing XLE in backwardation, pricing elevated near-term risk despite the bullish flow. This creates a compelling setup where technical momentum meets options positioning ahead of economic data that could shift energy demand expectations. SPY's -$2.5B negative gamma environment transforms tomorrow's NFP from routine data into a potential volatility explosion, with dealers forced to amplify any directional move through delta hedging. The massive April 1 expiry flow — 389K calls at 658 strike versus 711K puts clustered at 654-655 strikes — suggests positioning for a binary outcome around current levels, with the gamma flip at $660.35 acting as the fulcrum for post-NFP price action.

Layer 1: Sentiment -8Layer 2: GEX -$2.5B SPYLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.36/0.87Layer 9: NFP tomorrow
